L1.area: System configuration initialization and deployment default settings

L2.description: A cryptographic configuration weakness exists where TLS certificate verification is not enforced by default for connections to external services including authentication servers, registries, and webhook endpoints. An attacker positioned between the application and these services could perform man-in-the-middle attacks to intercept or modify sensitive communications such as authentication tokens.

L3.area: Default system configuration setup for new deployments, specifically the initialization of security-related connection settings
L3.description: A cryptographic configuration weakness exists where TLS certificate verification for OpenID Connect, LDAP, SAML, webhook, and registry server connections is not enabled by default during initial deployment setup. A network-positioned attacker could perform man-in-the-middle attacks against these services to intercept or tamper with authentication credentials and other sensitive data.

ground_truth.reason: TLS certificate verification is disabled by default for connections to authentication servers (OIDC, LDAP, SAML), registries, and webhook endpoints, allowing man-in-the-middle attacks to intercept or modify sensitive communications.
